reviews.review_description:
I usually use OVR permanently, simply to show an overlay of what the battery percentage level is. The one thing I miss though is that on all the technical statistics it can show the one obvious omission is a real time clock. If I'm playing something and know I have to quit at 20:15 I don't want to have to keep starting/stopping the game to see the clock on the Meta menu bar. It'd be much easier if the time were always visible as an overlay.

But I'm scoring it 5 as it's a fantastic tool anyway :-)
I don’t think this app is bad, but I don’t want it to be permanently on my screen. Couldn’t delete it.
Best thing is that you can see your battery

developer_response.body:
Thank you for using OVRMetricsTool and sharing your feedback.

When you uninstall the app, it is removed from your Quest Library. If you still see the app listed, what remains is an empty system stub. This artifact is left by the operating system due to permission requirement for this App. Rest assured, this stub is harmless and does not affect your device or user experience in any way.

reviews.review_description:
Good app. I would like an option to have the time on there tho, since it's hassle to take the headset off to look at a watch.
UPDATE 2025.08.03. It has started to mess up the battery level now, showing very inaccurate amounts. Tried reinstalling but it's still doing it. Annoying, because that is the main reason I use it.

reviews.review_title: I use it all the time
reviews.review_description: it's so good for a content creater bc every time I say me preds they think I'm lying
The battery indicator has not been showing the current battery level for quite some time now. This makes it completely useless.

developer_response.body: Thank you for reporting this issue with the battery indicator. We appreciate your feedback and wanted to let you know that the problem has been fixed. The battery indicator will be functioning correctly in the next OS release. Thanks again for helping us improve the experience!
